title: Desoldering Tool
body_html:
there are several common styles of desoldering tools commonly available that allow the use of a regular Soldering Iron for desoldering:
  • Plunger-style tools use a spring-loaded syringe to suck molten solder away from a joint (I personally have yet to find one of these that works terribly well)
  • Bulb-style tools have a rubber bulb that can be squished/let go to suck away solder. (I had a red one from Rat Shack and it worked ok. Having to pump it sucked, and made it easy to blow solder into a joint instead of sucking it away.)

You can make a superior DIY desoldering tool by cutting a bulb-style sucker in half and duct-taping it to a vacuum cleaner. Not having to pump the bulb rocks.

title: Soldering Iron
body_html:
Soldering Irons come in all shapes and sizes. Some basic guidelines:
  • Generally, a grounded iron (3-prong) will work better than an ungrounded one.
  • You don't need a shitload of power. 15-25W will be plenty.
  • Get as small of a tip as you can because you will probably need it :)
  • Temperature controlled irons are handy to have, but totally unnecessary for the beginner.

Learning To Solder is something that takes practice. It is highly advised to practice your first soldering on something you do not care about, as it takes some time and patience to get the hang of it. The ECU you need to run your car to get to work tomorrow is not the appropriate first thing to try to solder.

title: Vtec
body_html: Vtec is honda's patented system for variable Cam Profiles. It effectively gives an engine two independent cam profiles. The idea is that the Low Cam has conditions optimal for low RPMs and the High Cam has conditions optimal for higher RPMs. Vtec motors generally have a broader powerband characterized by two "humps" as opposed to one hump with non-vtec motors. The ideal Vtec Crossover Point can be found by superimposing a dyno graph of the Low Cam and High Cam and seeing where they cross.

title: Throttle Body
body_html:
The throttle body is the housing that contains the Throttle Plate, Idle Air Control Valve, and Map Sensor. The throttle cable is also connected to this. The throttle body essentially limits how much air is allowed to enter the engine, thereby regulating it's speed.

When fully opened, so that the Throttle Plate is parallel with the intake air stream, the area of the open trottle body becomes the limiting factor on how much air the engine can take in. This isn't usually a problem unless you have a highly modified race engine. In these applications, a larger throttle body ''MAY'' open up the possiblity for more top-end power.

title: OBD1 Conversion Formulae
body_html:
• OBD1_8bit Low Cam RPM - eight bit values used for low-cam tables and VTEC crossover points
  • OBD1_8bit High Cam RPM - eight bit values used for high-cam tables
  • OBD1_16bit RPM - 16-bit RPMs used throughout the code (including revlimits, etc)
  • OBD1_8bit MBar - Absolute pressure scale used in all tables
  • OBD1_8bit Fuel - Fuel values used in all tables
  • OBD1_8bit Advance - Ignition advance used in all tables

title: Real Time Programming
body_html:
Used for tuning your ecu and making changes to it on the fly.

Hardware

  • Easy-RTP v1.0 - Dave's single sided adaptation of Doc's original DS1230Y based design.
  • Easy-RTP Installation - Hardware mods for installation of the Easy-RtpV1.0 board on an OBD1 ECU.
  • Replacing the M5128 RAM chip - Replacing the 5128 XRAM 2K SRAM chip by a DS1220 NVSRAM (another RTP method, not yet finished)

title: Fuel Cut
body_html:
A Fuel Cut is when fuel to the motor is shut off completely.

This is usually done as part of a Rev Limiter, Launch Control or Boost Cut routine. Fuel Cut Rev Limiters are thought to be somewhat scary, as the engine may run lean from lack of fuel.

Spark Cut is not performed by the stock ECU, but many aftermarket ignitions use it. Spark Cut Rev Limiters are thought to be somewhat less harsh.

title: JDM
body_html:
Japanese Domestic Market - cars produced for use in Japan

Compare to: EDM, USDM

Note: JDM OBD1 Civic Integra ECUs generally lack the Electrical Load Detector and Pressure Atmosphere sensors (compared to USDM ECUs). They generally have more surface mount components, and use a smaller square case. They also generally have a ~112mph Speed Limiter.

title: Launch Control
body_html:
Launch control refers to a "two-step" revlimiter.

The way this works operationally is fairly simple - there is a lower Rev Limiter while the car is stationary, so that you can floor it without causing danger to the engine. Pop the clutch, the car starts moving and the low Rev Limiter is disabled, allowing you to drive the car normally.

The main point of Launch Control is to enable consistent launches by keeping the car at the same RPM each launch.
  • addExtrafeatures.js:

title: OBD1P13 Auto Manual
body_html:
Most Auto Ecus have a jumper at RP11 and have a blank space at RP12. Move RP11 to RP12 and you now have a Manual ECU. Here a closeup of the JDM Jumper configuration: Auto Jumpers

R96, C49 and R82 seem to be related to some Auto trans controls but can be left in place.

Some ECUs (Like the JDM P13-900) have resistors in both places. You can just remove both of them and replace RP12 with a jumper wire.

This applies to all ECUs that share the same board (P11, P12, P13, P14 and P39)

title: Kehein Indiana Precision Technology
body_html:
Keihin Indiana Precision Technology (IPT) appear to have designed the Honda ECUs. They have a website.

J. Wong: on a tangent, Indiana Precision Technology / Carolina System Technology does the latest Honda ECU's... i don't know where they are... Indiana? one of the Carolinas?

A. Burke: Indeed many (all?) recent Honda cars/trucks may have Keihin ECUs, however the ECU (Honda calls it "ECM/PCM") for the '98 Accord was manufactured by Motorola.

title: OBD1 8bit Fuel
body_html:
Given table value ''v'' and multiplier value ''m'', the fuel value as seen in a ROM editor is:

Fuel(''v'',''m'') = ''v*m''/4

The result is presumably proportional to the amount of fuel entering the engine. It has an effective range of 0-16256.25 -- so how does this relate to injector duty cycle?

Pulse width (ms) = PW = (''v*m''/4)/100 + 0.60ms = ''v*m''/400 + 0.60ms

Max Duty Cycle is (((1000*60*2/rpm)*0.9ms)

Duty Cycle = PW/TRPM = (''v*m''/400 + 0.60)/(60000/''RPM'')

Simplifying,

Duty Cycle = ''RPM''*(''v*m'' + 240)/24000000

This is based upon information provided by Didier Pelegri and not verified by myself.

--AndySloane

title: ECU-Code Compatibility
body_html:
Most honda ECUs can run code from other ECUs of a similar age, and similar family.

Civics and Integras generally can share code. Preludes and Accords are certainly from the same design team, and sometimes can share code.

For specific information consult:
  • OBD0 Code Compatibility - addresses OBD0 MPFI ECUs
  • OBD1 Code Compatibility - addresses OBD1 Civic Integra ECUs

title: OBD1 Civ Teg Routines
body_html:
Some routines have already been commented for your viewing pleasure.

P30 (203)

  • Doc ECUSchool Doc explains some of how IO is performed looking at the JDM P30 203 ROM. (This does apply to other OBD1 Civic Integra ECUs too.)
  • 203_AC - 203 JDM P30 AC routine
  • ACCut Routine - Cuts A/C under certain high-load conditions

title: Speed Limiter
body_html:
A speed limiter limits the top speed of a car. In almost all hondas, a Speed Limiter will be implemented as a piece of code that reads the Vehicle Speed Sensor and cuts fuel above a certain speed. JDM ECUs are famous for having speed limiters.

If you want to go faster than 180kph without reprogramming ECU you can use Hondainfo's Speed Limit Defencer device.

Hondainfo Speed Limit Defencer

title: Ecu Backup Processor
body_html: Honda ECUs are engineered with many levels of redundancy. There is a Backup processor (labeled NEC BACK0004 in most OBD0 and Oki 6534 in most OBD1 (??)). The backup processor is resposible for running the car when the ECU has detected a severe fault, as usually indicated by a "solid" CEL. It does not use the full array of sensors that the car has, and is intended to minimally allow the car to move under its own power in the event of a "total" failure of main ECU logic.

title: AC Cut Routine
body_html:
Author: evo (61.88.2.177) Date: 03-04-03 22:15

After much thought, ive decided to share my Air-Conditioner Cutoff routine.

This routine cuts out the a/c under a specified rpm OR TPS level.

JBR off 11.2, 4320 ; Jump if A/C switch is Off J XXXX ; Hook Code located at XXXX

LC A, 6D8D ; Load Target rpm
CMP AC, A ; Compare Actual rpm to Target rpm
JLT 6D8A ; Jump if Actual rpm < Target
LCB A, 6D8F ; Load Target TPS
CMPB 6F, A ; Compare Actual TPS to Target TPS
JGT 6D8A ; Jump if Actual TPS > Target
SB off 26.4
J 430C
J 432B ; Jump to Cut A/C

The Code is located around the 4300 offset in the P30 203

title: Compression Ratio
body_html:
Compression ratio refers to how much the air-fuel mixture is compressed during the compression stroke.

There are many ways to measure compression - Static Compression, Dynamic Compression, Piston Compression.

Dave Blundell says "HELP!!! I'm in over my head explaining this properly... Someone else do it"

title: Electrical Load Detector
body_html:
Electronic Load Detector

measures electrical load - used to determine if the alternator should be in low or high output mode.

Since Hondas are running so lean at idle, virtually any load will cause the idle to drop. Even small electrical loads, such as the turn signals, will cause the idle to fluctuate. The electrical load detector (ELD) was added to later model Hondas to monitor for any significant electrical loads. It sends a warning signal to the ECM before the load has a chance to effect the idle. The ECM makes slight adjustments to the idle air control (IAC) valve, injector PW, and ignition timing to compensate for the electrical load.

title: EA
body_html:
_EA (EA Prime) is a pin on the MCU that controls whether the MCU runs off Internal ROM or an external ROM chip.

_EA is pin 31 on 40-pin Intel8051 MCUs

J1 on OBD1 ECUs controls the state of _EA, and is used to activate an external ROM you install.

title: Piggy Back
body_html:
A Piggy Back controller is a device that "piggybacks" on top of the ECU. Piggybacks intercept signals coming from the engine and alter them before they reach the ECU.

The most commonly abused piggyback controllers are the Apexi SAFC, VAFC, SAFC2 and VAFC2. The Greddy E-Manage is a big badass piggyback controller on steroids. You could think of a lot of the garbage that comes with a JRSC kit as a piggyback, as their relay and crap setup causes bogus sensor values to be passed to the ECU.

title: RTP
body_html:
Real Time Programmability

The ability to change the program/tuning of an ECU while the ECU is running the motor. Currently, only the Zdyne SECU Gold and the AEM EMS Standalone support this.

Achieving real time programmability with a stock ECU is a significant goal for the PGMFI project.

title: Dual Tables
body_html:
Dual tables simply refers to there being two sets of fuel/timing information in a single ROM. Vtec is an example of this - there is one set of tuning information for the Low Cam and one for the High Cam. Additionally, the ECUNitrous Control plans to include two sets of tuning information so that the ECU can tune for nitrous.

See also: Dual Roms

title: O2 Heater Circuit
body_html: the O2 heater circuit is what makes 4-wire Oxygen Sensors different from 1-wire sensors. The heater circuit helps the sensor stay in its optimum temperature range so that it reads accurately. See One Wire To4 Wire O2 Sensor for information on adding circuitry to an ECU to support the O2 Heater Circuit. See the Rom Maps for information on diabling the code 41 present when it is absent/malfunctioning.

title: Latch
body_html:
to latch something is to hold or preserve its state

latches, like the 74 HC373 are logic gates that hold the state of IO lines.

Library.Latch moved from Library.latch on 05 Mar 2004 - 17:40 by Home.blundar - put it back

title: UV-Erase
body_html:
many chips have a clear glass window in the top of them. If you expose them to short UV (i.e. the kind that causes instant cancer) from a sterlizer lamp or UV-eraser bulb for sufficient time, the information stored on the chip is erased and it can be re-used.

Note: "blacklights" that hippies like to use in their rooms are not reliable for erasing chips.

Note #2: sunlight is not reliable for erasing chips.

Note #3: this link explains how to build an eraser.

title: Blue Loctite
body_html:
Blue Loc-Tite is like a girlfriend... your stuck with her, but you can get rid of her if you want to. Unlike Red Loctite.

You put Blue Loc-Tite on a bolt that is prone to losening from vibration, or one that you don't really want coming loose, but it may be a little more work to get the bolt out later. I've had the Tps Sensor loosen on me twice before, so it is a good candidate.

Now if it is seriously critical that the part not come loose, then you want Red Loctite.

title: Desoldering Tips
body_html: Some basic wisdom from my time with the desoldering tools: #DO NOT USE THE SUCTION ALL THE TIME #BE PATIENT. You need to wait for the solder to melt all the way through before sucking otherwise you'll be left with stuff to clean up #Rocking back and forth slowly (~1hz) often works well for difficult spots #The amount of heat you need is directly proportional to the amount of copper trace material surrounding the pin. You'll find that pin 15 gets stuck a lot as it is in a big fucking copper ground trace #Solder braid is your friend. Its cheap, and if used right will make your desoldering effort a very clean one.

title: Maf Sensor
body_html: The Mass Air-Flow Sensor directly measures the mass of the air entering the engine. This is accomplished using either a flapper-valve which is deflected a certain angle by the mass of the air as it flows through it, or with a heated wire element in the air-stream that changes resistance based on temperature. The more mass of air that flows across the heated element, the more the element cools down, and thus the less resistance it measures. The Maf Sensor is quite different than the Map Sensor used in all PGMFI systems.

title: EPROM
body_html:
Electrically Programmable ROM

ROM that can be programmed using a Rom Burner

The most common types pin-compatible with most OBD0 and OBD1 Honda ECUs are the 27 C256 and the 29 C256. Note, the 28 C256 is not pin-compatible.

title: Hex File
body_html: A "hex" file is a file encoded in Intel Hex format. The Intel HEX file is an ASCII text file with lines of text that follow the Intel HEX file format. Each line in an Intel HEX file contains one HEX record. These records are made up of hexadecimal numbers that represent machine language code and/or constant data. Intel HEX files are often used to transfer the program and data that would be stored in a ROM or EPROM. Most EPROM programmers or emulators can use Intel HEX files.

title: Cam Profile
body_html: A cam profile is a distinct shape of a Cam Shaft. Cam Lift, Cam Duration and Cam Overlap are generally the three things measured on a Cam Shaft.

title: Red Loctite
body_html:
Red Loc-Tite is like a wife... your stuck with her and to get rid of her, it's gonna cost you a bundle. Unlike Blue Loctite.

You put Red Loc-Tite on a bolt that is prone to losening from vibration and also would lead to catastrophic relusts if it did. Flywheel bolt is a good example but you will have to use some major effort to get the bolt out later, so beforewarned. If it is isn't so critical but you still want to make sure it won't just vibrate loose, then you want Blue Loctite.

title: ECU Nitrous Control
body_html: ppl are working on a ROM where the ECU activates the nitrous solenoid instead of the usual WOT switch. The main advantage of this is that the ECU knows when nitrous is active, and can therefore switch to a different set of fuel/timing maps. Dry kit + 450cc injectors + ECUNitrous Control w/ Dual Tables = FINE TUNED wet kit!

title: PM8
body_html:
The PM8 came in the US on 88-91 CRX HF. These ECUs always lacked external ROMs, and they seem to be just a wee lil bit different than PM6/PM7 in terms of hardware configuration. They are much more similar to the PM6/PM7 than the PR4

-- The Admin - 18 Dec 2003

title: P05
body_html:
P05

92-95 OBD1 Civic CX (D15B8)

The P05 ECU came with the D15 SOHC non-vtec motor with close-coupled cat and a 1-wire O2 sensor in the 92-95 Civic CX. Basically an OBD1 version of the 88-91 HF motor. FUEL ECONOMY!!!

Heres a pic i took from a Stock P05.

title: Electronic Advance
body_html:
Electronic Advance refers to a motor setup where Spark Advance can be controlled electronically by the ECU. Electronic Advance replaced Vacuum Advance because it is much more flexible for tuning, and allows much better control of the motor.

Distributorless direct-fire distributorless ignitions are largely replacing electronic advance now (early 2004).

title: Ignitor Replacement
body_html:
It is possible to replace the ignitor in the distributor with a solid state circuit to trigger the input of a MSD/Crane/etc.

Dave:

Note that the ZTX857 is not an absolute requirement. It's just that it was the same size as the transistor it replaced. You could use any power Darlington NPN that can handle a couple amps.

title: 5128XRAM
body_html:
5128 XRAM explanation :

The 5128 chip present in OBD0 ECUs is a 2K byte(?) SRAM chip. It is mapped into memory space at 1XXX hex - any external communication(MOVX A,@DPTR) with 1000 <= DPTR < 2000h will address the SRAM chip.

see OBD0 PM6 PM7 RAM_Locations for some documentation on what is stored in XRAM.

title: Spark Cut
body_html: A spark cut is when fuel continues to be supplied to the motor but no sparks are triggered to ignite the fuel. This is a good way to shoot flames out of the back of a hot exhaust. Spark Cuts are generally implemented by aftermarket ignition systems. The stock ECU uses a Fuel Cut for Rev Limiters and Speed Limiters.

title: Conversion
body_html:
Describe Conversion here.

Conversion is the act of modifying the Wire Harness at the ECU so you can fit ohter Generation (OBD) ECU. For example, you might want to run an OBD1 ECU into a factory OBD2 car.

title: Saturated Injectors
body_html:
Saturated Injectors aka High Impedance Injectors

Impedance typically 12-16ohms. At 12volts, this is a draw of ~1-.75 amps. The low current helps keep the injectors cool.

The saturated injectors see a steady 12volts, and ground is modulated to produce pulsing.

These are the stock style of injector for OBD1 and up hondas, so no resistor box is needed.

title: OBD0 Vtec EC Us
body_html:
The OBD0 VTEC ECU's are the PR3 and the PW0. These ECUs ran the B16A1 in OBD0 cars from 89-91. There is no code interchangeability between the two ECU's.

They use a crystal that operates at 7.900 MHZ

These ECUs presently arent getting much love from the development folks.

title: SOHC
body_html:
Single ~OverHead Cam motors have a single Cam Shaft that opens both Intake Valves and Exhaust Valves.

Most "D" series motors are SOHC.

See also: DOHC

title: MAX233

the Maxim MAX233 chip is commonly used to translate between TTL and RS232 levels during serial communication. It does not require any external capacitors, unlike the MAX232.

title: API
body_html:
Application Programming Interface

An API is a set of functions (usually present in a DLL or other code library) that you can call in order to perform taks. Some common APIs include the Microsoft Win32 API, the X windows API, and the Crome Script API.

title: Boost Controller
body_html:
A boost controller is a device that allows the levels of boost generated by a turbo to be controlled. NOTE: Boost controllers can only raise boost levels above the level set by your Waste Gate!

See also Electronic Boost Controller and ECUBoost Controller

title: Oki80C154
body_html: MCU used in OBD0 MPFI ECUs that has zero internal code memory. requires the use of an external EPROM It is mostly 8051 compatible, and features a third timer like many other replacements. The main difference from a pure 8051 revolves around how the A5 instruction is handled.

title: 91PM6
body_html:
Describe 91 PM6 here.

  • 91 PM6 Target Idle describes how to set the target idle in a PM6. Similar to 91 PM7 Target Idle
  • 91 PM6_ECU_LED_CEL describes the blinking of the LED on the ECU.

title: uncorked
body_html: The stock code divides the Map Sensor reading by two. This caps the maximum pressure the ECU can respond to by half the maximum of what the Map Sensor is capable of reading. By simply removing this division, we have uncorked the Map Sensor to read all the way up to 11psi of boost.
